properties.remark: Delayed Report. An Ice Jam developed on Feb 29th around 9:30AM. It broke on Mar 4th around 10:30AM. Photos via social media showed a jam. Time estimated based on MXLM1 River Gage data. No flooding reported. Estimated length of the ice jam was 2.5 river miles.
Small ice jam on the Meduxnekeag River near the Tate and Lyle Plant in Houlton.
150 foot ice jam on the Little Madawaska River. No flooding associated with the jam.
Ice Jam has developed downstream of the Caribou Dam near Limestone St and River Road in Caribou. No flooding noticeable due to low natural flows.
Ice jammed up approximately 2 miles upstream of the Dickey Bridge.
An Ice Jam has developed at the Dickey Bridge in Allagash. This is confirmed by Maine DOT Camera and USGS Gage along with Public Reports. Ice Jam is stuck near the bridge. No flooding reported.
USGS Gage at Nine Mile shows an Ice Jam has developed downstream of the gage causing a 6 foot spike in readings.
The St. John river ice has an ice jam at the confluence with the Allagash., extending upstream 1.5 miles to around the Catholic Church.
Delayed Report via social media with photos. A small Ice Jam has flooded the private Gardner Creek camp road, which is common each year along the Aroostook River banks.
1/2 mile ice jam in place just past Daigle Island on the St John River.
ice jam in place just below the gage on the Allagash River.

About 100 feet of broken ice.

Photos via social media shows an ice jam on the Little Madawaska River at the Little Madawaska Lake Road in Westmanland. Photos show broken jumbled ice generally 3-5 inches in thickness. Jam is approx 1/4-1/2 mile long. Flow is elevated but no apparent flooding.

An Ice Jam has developed on the St. John River caused by Quisbis Island near the Grand Isle townline in the Notre Dame village. Minor flooding of Farmlands in the flood plain upstream of the jam in Lille. The jam is approx 1/2mi long and consists of chunked frazil ice with estimated thickness of 3-6 inches.

Dover-Foxcroft Police Department confirms an Ice Jam has developed approx 1/2mi downstream of the USGS River Gage (DOVM1) at Lowes Covered Bridge. This gage went from 4.48ft at 12AM to 7.05ft at 2AM. The police department reported water levels behind the jam have come up about 1 foot. No flood concerns at this time.
